evolutionCost: 25 candy for Haunter + 100 candy for Gengar = 125 total. Buddy distance is 3km/candy.
shinyRate: Shiny is white. A shiny Gengar is white—one of the best-looking ones.

origin: Haunter is the middle evolution of Gastly. It has icy hands that cause chills, and if its tongue touches someone, they will shake uncontrollably. Its name comes from 'haunt' + 'er'.
goRelevance: A stepping stone to Gengar. Pure Haunter can be used in Little/Great League as an alternative, though Gengar is always better.
battleTip: Haunter with Shadow Claw + Shadow Ball + Sludge Bomb is a weaker version of Gengar. Use it if you don't have enough candy.
easterEgg: Haunter is the featured Pokémon in the 'Ghost of Maiden's Peak' episode of the anime—one of the scariest episodes of the original series.
goDifference: In the main series, Haunter requires a trade to evolve into Gengar. In GO, it costs 100 candy.
evolutionCost: 100 candy to evolve into Gengar. Buddy distance is 3km/candy.
shinyRate: White shiny.

origin: Gengar is the final form of Gastly. Its appearance is inspired by urban shadows and hauntings. It is said to lurk near the weak to drain their life force. Name derived from 'gangster' + 'phantom'.
goRelevance: TOP-tier Ghost-type. Shadow Claw + Shadow Ball + Sludge Bomb. ATK 261. Mega Gengar is the best Ghost in the game with 349 ATK. Rank #6 in Master League.
battleTip: Gengar with Legacy move Lick + Shadow Ball is even better than Shadow Claw. If you have one with Lick, it's a treasure. Mega Gengar is fragile but deadly.
easterEgg: Gengar is Clefable's shadow! Compare their designs—same shape, pointed ears, rounded body. Gengar is the 'shadow' version.
goDifference: In the main series, Gengar mega evolves. In GO, Mega Gengar was added and is the strongest Ghost-type in the game.
evolutionCost: 100 candy from Haunter. Buddy 3km/candy. 296 XL candy to max out. Mega Gengar: 200 Mega Energy.
shinyRate: White shiny. Shiny Mega Gengar is white with red eyes—scary and beautiful.

origin: Onix is inspired by stone snakes and minerals. Its body consists of giant rocks held together by magnetic attraction. Its name comes from 'onyx,' a gemstone.
goRelevance: Common in caves. Onix has 232 DEF but only 85 ATK—one of the most unbalanced stats in the game. Evolves into Steelix using a Metal Coat.
battleTip: Steelix (Gen 2) with Dragon Tail + Crunch + Earthquake is a great Great League tank. Its 333 DEF (Mega) makes it nearly impenetrable.
easterEgg: Onix is 8.8 meters long, making it one of the longest Pokémon. However, it only weighs 210kg, which is quite light for its size.
goDifference: In the main series, Onix requires a trade while holding a Metal Coat to evolve. In GO, it costs 50 Candy + a Metal Coat (special item).
evolutionCost: 50 Candy + Metal Coat to evolve into Steelix. 5km per Buddy Candy.
shinyRate: Shiny Onix is light green. Shiny Steelix is gold.

origin: Drowzee is inspired by tapirs and the Baku (a Japanese mythological creature that eats nightmares). It sleeps constantly. Its name comes from 'drowse' and 'zee' (zzz for sleep).
goRelevance: Common in urban areas. Hypno is a defensive Psychic-type with a solid Great League presence. Runs Hypnosis, Psychic, and Shadow Ball.
battleTip: Hypno with Confusion + Thunder Punch + Shadow Ball provides coverage against Dark and Ghost-types. Shadow Ball is great for surprising opponents.
easterEgg: Drowzee and the Baku—the Baku is a Japanese folklore creature that eats nightmares. Drowzee eats dreams, not nightmares.
goDifference: In the main series, Drowzee learns Hypnosis at level 1. In GO, Hypnosis is a fast move that Drowzee can learn.
evolutionCost: 50 candy to evolve into Hypno. 3km per candy as a buddy.
shinyRate: Light pink shiny. Hypno is also pink.

origin: Hypno is the evolved form of Drowzee. It holds a pendulum to hypnotize people. Legend says it once led children to unknown places. Its name comes from 'hypnosis' + 'no'.
goRelevance: A popular defensive Psychic-type in Great League PvP. Confusion + Thunder Punch + Shadow Ball provides coverage against almost everything. Ranked #162 on PvPoke.
battleTip: Hypno with Thunder Punch and Fire Punch covers Steel and Dark-types. Its versatile moveset is its main advantage.
easterEgg: In the main series Pokédex, Hypno is described as a child kidnapper—one of the most disturbing entries in the Nintendo franchise.
goDifference: In the main series, Hypno learns Dream Eater and Nightmare. In GO, these moves don't exist because status effects work differently.
evolutionCost: 50 Drowzee Candy. 3km buddy distance per candy.
shinyRate: Pink shiny variant.

origin: Krabby is inspired by fiddler crabs. Its pincers regrow if lost. Name is 'crab' + 'by'. One of the most classic crab designs in Pokémon.
goRelevance: Common near beaches and coastal areas. Kingler is a Water-type with 240 ATK—very high for its tier. Runs Metal Claw + X-Scissor + Crabhammer.
battleTip: Kingler with Mud Shot + Crabhammer + X-Scissor generates energy extremely fast. Its 240 ATK is devastating in Great League.
easterEgg: Krabby was one of Ash's Pokémon in the Orange Islands—a rare case where Ash caught something that wasn't Normal or Flying-type.
goDifference: In the main series, Krabby can learn Guillotine (an OHKO move). In GO, OHKO moves do not exist.
evolutionCost: 50 candy to evolve into Kingler. 3km/candy buddy distance.
shinyRate: Shiny Krabby is light blue. Shiny Kingler is dark blue.

origin: Kingler is the evolved form of Krabby. Its right claw is disproportionately large and strong enough to crush rocks. Its name comes from 'king' + 'crab' + 'ler'.
goRelevance: 240 ATK—higher than many 'strong' Water-types. Mud Shot + Crabhammer + X-Scissor is a high-pressure combo. Viable in Great League.
battleTip: Kingler with Mud Shot + Crabhammer + X-Scissor provides coverage against Grass-types (a Water weakness) and Psychic-types.
easterEgg: Gigantamax Kingler (Gen 8) has a 30-meter claw that acts like a parasol. It is one of the most absurd designs.
goDifference: In the main series, Kingler learns Vice Grip and Crabhammer. In GO, Crabhammer is a powerful Water-type charged move.
evolutionCost: 50 Krabby candy. Buddy distance: 3km/candy.
shinyRate: Dark blue shiny.

origin: Voltorb is inspired by Poke Balls that came to life. Legend says they were created when a Poke Ball was struck by lightning. Named from 'volt' + 'orb'. Hisuian variant is Electric/Grass.
goRelevance: Common in industrial and urban areas. Electrode is a pure Electric-type with balanced stats. Rarely used in PvP.
battleTip: Electrode with Volt Switch + Foul Play + Thunderbolt provides coverage against Dark and Ghost-types.
easterEgg: Voltorb and Electrode are known for exploding when irritated. In the main series, they have Self-Destruct as a signature move.
goDifference: In the main series, Voltorb can learn Self-Destruct and Explosion. In GO, these moves would be suicidal, so they were omitted.
evolutionCost: 50 candy to evolve into Electrode. Buddy distance is 3km/candy.
shinyRate: Shiny is red (like an inverted Poke Ball). Very striking.

origin: Electrode is the evolved form of Voltorb. It resembles an inverted Poké Ball and is known for being unstable and prone to exploding. Its name comes from 'electrode'.
goRelevance: A fast Electric-type with Volt Switch + Foul Play. Decent for Great League, but outclassed by Galvantula and Lanturn.
battleTip: Electrode with Foul Play surprises opponents expecting pure Electric moves. Dark-type coverage is rare for Electric-types.
easterEgg: Electrode is the fastest Pokémon from the original games (140 base Speed in main series). In GO, Speed is not a direct stat.
goDifference: In the main series, Electrode is known for exploding (Self-Destruct). In GO, it simply does not have Explosion as a viable move.
evolutionCost: 50 Voltorb Candy. 3km buddy distance per candy.
shinyRate: Dark red shiny.

origin: Exeggcute is inspired by a clutch of 6 eggs. Despite their appearance, they are actually seeds that look like eggs. One of the most confusing Pokémon designs. The name comes from 'egg' + 'execute'.
goRelevance: Common in grassy areas and Sunny weather. Exeggutor is Grass/Psychic with a high 233 ATK. The Alolan variant (Grass/Dragon) is also popular.
battleTip: Alolan Exeggutor (Grass/Dragon) is unique. It has a 4x weakness to Ice. However, its high ATK and moves like Bullet Seed + Draco Meteor are quite strong.
easterEgg: Exeggcute aren't eggs; they are seeds that look like eggs. The Pokédex insists on this, but nobody believes it. We keep calling them eggs.
goDifference: In the main series, Exeggcute needs a Leaf Stone to evolve. In GO, it costs 50 Candy.
evolutionCost: 50 Candy to evolve into Exeggutor. Buddy distance is 3km/candy. Alolan Exeggcute: 50 Candy + Leaf Stone? No—just 50 Candy for Alolan Exeggutor in GO.
shinyRate: Shiny is light pink. Shiny Exeggutor is also pink.

origin: Exeggutor is the evolved form of Exeggcute. One head acts as the leader while the other three follow. The Alolan variant is a 10-meter-tall DRAGON palm tree. Name comes from 'egg' + 'executor'.
goRelevance: Grass/Psychic with 233 ATK. Runs Bullet Seed + Seed Bomb + Psychic. The Alolan variant (Grass/Dragon) is better for raids due to its unique typing.
battleTip: Alolan Exeggutor is the tallest Pokémon in the game (10.9m). It has a 4x weakness to Ice, but Bullet Seed + Draco Meteor provides high DPS in raids.
easterEgg: Alolan Exeggutor stands 10.9 meters tall. In the anime, it appears so tall that it goes off-screen. It is an intentional visual gag.
goDifference: In the main series, Exeggutor can learn Stomp and Egg Bomb. In GO, Egg Bomb was replaced by Seed Bomb.
evolutionCost: 50 Exeggcute candy. 3km/candy buddy distance.
shinyRate: Standard shiny is pink. Alolan Exeggutor shiny is gold.

origin: Cubone is inspired by cubs that wear their deceased mother's skull as a helmet. One of the saddest stories in the Pokémon universe. Tears form the marks on the skull. Name comes from 'cub' + 'bone'.
goRelevance: Common in dry and mountainous areas. Marowak is a Ground-type with Bone Club + Bonemerang. The Alolan variant (Fire/Ghost) is much more popular.
battleTip: Alolan Marowak with Fire Spin + Shadow Bone + Bone Club is top-tier in Great and Ultra League. Shadow Bone is an excellent exclusive move.
easterEgg: Cubone's story is confirmed to be sad: its mother dies and it wears the skull. However, some say it is a Kangaskhan skull—a popular theory.
goDifference: In the main series, Cubone evolves at level 28. In GO, it costs 50 candy.
evolutionCost: 50 candy for Marowak. Buddy 3km/candy. Alolan Cubone: 50 candy for Alolan Marowak.
shinyRate: Light green shiny. Alolan Marowak shiny is dark purple.

origin: Marowak is the evolved form of Cubone. Having overcome the loss of its mother, it is now a fierce warrior. The Alolan variant (Fire/Ghost) is a flaming spirit. Its name comes from 'marrow' + 'wak' (likely from 'whack').
goRelevance: A mid-tier Ground-type. Alolan Marowak is the real standout—a Fire/Ghost type with the exclusive Shadow Bone. It is top-tier in Great League PvP.
battleTip: Alolan Marowak with Fire Spin + Shadow Bone + Bone Club provides coverage against Ghost, Psychic, Steel, and Grass-types. Very versatile.
easterEgg: Alolan Marowak is Fire/Ghost because the full moon of Alola caused the mother's spirit to protect her child with fire.
goDifference: In the main series, Marowak can learn Thrash and Double-Edge. In GO, Thrash does not exist as a move.
evolutionCost: 50 Cubone candy. 3km buddy distance per candy.
shinyRate: The shiny is lighter in color. Shiny Alolan Marowak is dark blue with purple flames.

origin: Hitmonlee is inspired by kickboxers and Bruce Lee. Its legs act like springs that stretch for kicks. Name is 'hit' + 'mon' (monster) + 'lee' (Bruce Lee).
goRelevance: Rare — obtained from Tyrogue (Gen 2). Fighting-type with Low Kick + Close Combat + Stone Edge. Used in Great League.
battleTip: Hitmonlee with Low Kick + Close Combat + Stone Edge covers Flying-types. ATK 224 is high, but DEF 181 and STA 137 are average.
easterEgg: Hitmonlee and Hitmonchan are a tribute to Bruce Lee (Lee) and Jackie Chan (Chan). Hitmontop is the tribute to the Hong Kong actor.
goDifference: In main series, Hitmonlee needs Tyrogue with ATK > DEF to evolve. In GO, Tyrogue evolves based on which stat is highest.
evolutionCost: 25 Tyrogue Candy. Tyrogue obtained from 5km eggs. Buddy 5km/candy.
shinyRate: Light green shiny. Subtle.

origin: Hitmonchan is inspired by boxers and Jackie Chan. Its fists are like dynamite and can throw punches at supersonic speeds. Its name comes from 'hit' + 'mon' + 'chan' (Jackie Chan).
goRelevance: A Fighting-type with Counter + Ice Punch + Thunder Punch. It offers excellent elemental coverage and is used in the Great League.
battleTip: A Hitmonchan with Ice Punch + Thunder Punch + Fire Punch covers Flying, Water, Grass, Ice, and Steel-types. It is rare to have such wide coverage.
easterEgg: Hitmonchan's bangs hide part of its face, inspired by the hairstyles of Japanese boxers.
goDifference: In the main series, Hitmonchan can learn Bulk Up and Mach Punch. In GO, status moves like Bulk Up are less impactful.
evolutionCost: 25 Tyrogue Candy. Buddy distance is 5km per candy.
shinyRate: Light green shiny.

origin: Lickitung is inspired by lizards and anteaters with long tongues. Its tongue is 2 meters long and can be used like a whip. It is sticky and flexible. Its name comes from 'lick' + 'tongue'.
goRelevance: Common in urban areas. Lickitung evolves into Lickilicky (Gen 4) using a Sinnoh Stone. Lickilicky is a decent Normal-type for the Great League.
battleTip: Lickilicky with Lick + Body Slam + Earthquake provides coverage against Steel, Fire, and Electric-types. Its 207 STA is quite high.
easterEgg: In Pokémon Amie, Lickitung can be taught to 'lick' instead of walking as an animation. It is one of the strangest animations.
goDifference: In the main series, Lickitung learns Rollout (a Rock-type move). In GO, Rollout exists as a Rock-type fast move.
evolutionCost: 50 Candy + Sinnoh Stone to evolve into Lickilicky. Buddy distance is 3km/candy.
shinyRate: Light pink shiny.

origin: Koffing is inspired by toxic gases and pollution balloons. These spheres emerge from chemical waste and float. Its name comes from 'coffin' + 'fuming'.
goRelevance: Common in urban and industrial areas. Weezing is a decent Poison-type. The Galarian variant (Fairy) is more widely used in PvP.
battleTip: A Weezing with Tackle + Sludge Bomb + Dark Pulse provides coverage against Psychic-types that counter pure Poison-types.
easterEgg: Koffing has been a Team Rocket Pokémon since the start. James and Meowth are also frequently defeated by it.
goDifference: In the main series, Koffing can learn Self-Destruct and Explosion. In GO, these moves are not available to it.
evolutionCost: 50 candy to evolve into Weezing. Buddy distance is 3km/candy. Galarian Koffing: 50 candy to evolve into Galarian Weezing.
shinyRate: Shiny is light blue. Shiny Weezing is blue.

origin: Weezing is the evolved form of Koffing—two Koffing joined together. Legend says Koffing rarely evolve naturally unless in highly polluted environments. Named after 'wheeze'. Galarian variant is Fairy-type.
goRelevance: A decent Poison-type for PvP. Galarian Weezing (Fairy) is used for its unique Fairy/Poison typing—great against Dragon and Fighting types.
battleTip: Galarian Weezing with Fairy Wind + Play Rough + Sludge Bomb provides coverage against Fighting, Dark, and Dragon-types.
easterEgg: Galarian Weezing has chimneys that emit pink smoke—but the smoke is actually pollution turned into fairy energy. An environmental critique.
goDifference: In the main series, Weezing learns Smokescreen (accuracy down). In GO, accuracy debuffs do not exist.
evolutionCost: 50 Koffing Candy. 3km per buddy candy.
shinyRate: Blue shiny. Galarian Weezing shiny is dark blue.

origin: Rhyhorn is inspired by armored rhinoceroses. It has a tiny brain and acts on instinct. Its name combines 'rhino' and 'horn'.
goRelevance: Common in dry and mountainous areas. Rhyperior (Gen 4) is Ground/Rock with 241 ATK and 224 DEF—a powerful offensive tank.
battleTip: Rhyperior with Mud-Slap + Rock Wrecker + Earthquake is devastating. Rock Wrecker is an exclusive move (2021 CD). Beware of 4x weakness to Water and Grass.
easterEgg: Rhyhorn can learn Horn Drill (OHKO move) in the main series. It is known for having one of the lowest intelligence stats of all Pokémon.
goDifference: In the main series, Rhyhorn evolves at level 42. In GO, it costs 50 Candy to evolve into Rhydon.
evolutionCost: 50 Candy for Rhydon + 100 Candy + Sinnoh Stone for Rhyperior. Buddy distance is 3km/candy.
shinyRate: Shiny is dark gray. Shiny Rhyperior is golden.

origin: Rhydon is the middle evolution of Rhyhorn. It can drill through rock with its horn. Its stone-like hide is nearly impenetrable. Name comes from 'rhino' + 'don' (lord).
goRelevance: Stepping stone to Rhyperior. Rhydon can be used as a Ground/Rock attacker with Earthquake + Stone Edge, but Rhyperior is much better.
battleTip: Rhydon with Mud-Slap + Earthquake + Surf provides coverage against Fire and Ground-types. Surf is rare coverage for a Rock/Ground type.
easterEgg: Rhydon was the FIRST Pokémon ever created by Game Freak designers. It is Pokémon #001 in order of creation.
goDifference: In the main series, Rhydon needs to be traded with a Protector to evolve. In GO, it costs 100 Candy + a Sinnoh Stone.
evolutionCost: 100 Candy + Sinnoh Stone to evolve into Rhyperior. Buddy distance: 3km/candy.
shinyRate: Dark grey shiny.

origin: Chansey is inspired by Pokémon Center nurses and eggs. It carries an egg to share with those in need. Its name comes from 'chance' + 'sey' (from nurse).
goRelevance: Rare. Blissey (Gen 2) has the highest STA in the game (496). The best gym defender in Pokémon GO. Put a Blissey in any gym and it's tough to take down.
battleTip: Chansey has 487 STA, the second highest in the game (behind Blissey's 496). Blissey with Zen Headbutt + Dazzling Gleam + Psychic is the ultimate gym defender.
easterEgg: Chansey is the Pokémon used by Nurse Joys in Pokémon Centers. Chansey's egg is highly nutritious and rejuvenating.
goDifference: In the main series, Chansey needs friendship to evolve into Blissey. In GO, it costs 50 candy.
evolutionCost: 50 candy to evolve into Blissey. 5km buddy distance per candy. Rare—Chansey spawns are very limited.
shinyRate: Shiny is light green. Shiny Blissey is also green.

origin: Tangela is inspired by seaweed and tangled vines. Blue vines cover its entire body, with only its red eyes visible. Its name comes from 'tangle'.
goRelevance: Common near lakes. Evolves into Tangrowth (Gen 4) using 100 Candy + Ancient Power.
battleTip: Tangrowth with Vine Whip + Power Whip + Rock Slide provides coverage against Fire and Flying-types. 183 ATK is decent for a Grass-type.
easterEgg: Tangela's vines are extremely durable and can be used as ropes, but they release a sticky latex if broken.
goDifference: In the main series, Tangela must learn Ancient Power and level up to evolve. In GO, it requires 100 Candy + Ancient Power.
evolutionCost: 100 Candy + must know Ancient Power to evolve into Tangrowth. Buddy distance: 3km/candy.
shinyRate: Shiny is light purple. Shiny Tangrowth is bluish.

origin: Kangaskhan is inspired by kangaroos with joeys in their pouches. The baby fights alongside its mother without leaving the pouch. Its name combines 'kangaroo' and 'khan' (leader). Exclusive to raids in the Australasia region.
goRelevance: Regional (Asia-Pacific). Normal-type with 181 ATK and 233 STA. It has a Mega Evolution with the Parental Bond ability that attacks twice.
battleTip: Mega Kangaskhan with Mud-Slap + Power-Up Punch + Return is great for raids and PvP. Parental Bond makes every attack hit twice.
easterEgg: Kangaskhan is one of the first regional Pokémon—in Pokémon GO, it is exclusive to certain parts of the world (Oceania).
goDifference: In the main series, Kangaskhan Mega Evolves. In GO, Mega Kangaskhan was added and is one of the best Normal-types.
evolutionCost: Does not evolve. Buddy 3km/candy. Kangaskhan's baby? The baby in the pouch never leaves—unlike Cubone, who loses its mother.
shinyRate: Light green shiny. Mega Kangaskhan is bluish.

origin: Horsea is inspired by seahorses and sea dragons. It can shoot black ink to escape. Its name comes from 'horse' + 'sea'.
goRelevance: Common in coastal areas. Kingdra (Gen 2) is Water/Dragon with excellent defensive typing (weak only to Dragon and Fairy).
battleTip: Kingdra with Dragon Breath + Hydro Pump + Octazooka provides coverage against Steel and Fire-types. Water/Dragon is one of the best typings in the game.
easterEgg: Horsea can shoot black ink to escape predators. It can also change color, much like a chameleon.
goDifference: In the main series, Horsea evolves at level 32. In GO, it costs 50 Candy.
evolutionCost: 50 Candy for Seadra + 100 Candy + Dragon Scale for Kingdra. Buddy distance is 3km/candy.
shinyRate: Shiny is light blue. Shiny Kingdra is purple.

origin: Seadra is the middle evolution of Horsea. It has poisonous spikes that cause paralysis. Its name comes from 'sea' + 'dragon' + 'adra' (hydra?).
goRelevance: Stepping stone to Kingdra. Pure Seadra is rarely used — Kingdra is always preferred.
battleTip: Seadra with Water Gun + Hydro Pump + Blizzard provides coverage against Grass and Dragon-types.
easterEgg: Seadra is based on real-life poisonous seahorses. Seadra's spikes can cause dizziness.
goDifference: In the main series, Seadra needs to be traded holding a Dragon Scale to evolve into Kingdra. In GO, it costs 100 candy + Dragon Scale.
evolutionCost: 100 candy + Dragon Scale for Kingdra. Buddy distance is 3km/candy.
shinyRate: Light blue shiny.

origin: Goldeen is inspired by goldfish and ornamental carp. Its fins are elegant like fans. The horn on its head is used in mating rituals. Its name comes from 'gold' + 'deen' (possibly from 'queen' or royalty).
goRelevance: Common in lakes and rivers with clean water. Seaking is a mid-tier Water-type that can run Poison Jab + Drill Run.
battleTip: Seaking with Poison Jab + Drill Run + Megahorn provides coverage against Grass and Electric-types, a rare trait for a Water-type.
easterEgg: In the anime, Goldeen was portrayed as a very elegant and graceful fish. Misty owned one that she used in beauty contests.
goDifference: In the main series, Goldeen can learn Waterfall as an HM. In GO, Waterfall is a fast move that Goldeen can learn.
evolutionCost: 50 candy to evolve into Seaking. Buddy distance is 3km/candy.
shinyRate: Shiny Goldeen is light pink. Shiny Seaking is olive green.

origin: Seaking is the evolved form of Goldeen. In autumn, its horn becomes more colorful to attract mates. Name comes from 'sea' + 'king'.
goRelevance: Mid-tier Water-type with Poison Jab + Drill Run. Rarely used in serious battles.
battleTip: Seaking with Megahorn surprises Grass-types. Bug-type coverage is rare among Water-types.
easterEgg: Seaking can learn Water Pulse in the main series. It is known for having one of the widest move pools among Water-types in GO.
goDifference: In the main series, Seaking learns Horn Drill (OHKO). In GO, OHKO moves do not exist.
evolutionCost: 50 Goldeen candy. Buddy 3km/candy.
shinyRate: Olive green shiny.

origin: Staryu is inspired by starfish. If its body is cut, it regenerates. The jewel in the center is its vital organ. Its name comes from 'star' + 'yu' (from 'you'?).
goRelevance: Common in coastal areas. Starmie is a Water/Psychic type with 210 ATK—great for raids and PvP. One of the best Gen 1 Water-types.
battleTip: Starmie with Water Gun + Hydro Pump + Psychic covers Fighting and Poison-types. Fast and versatile.
easterEgg: Staryu and Starmie share a starfish design and star symbol. The central jewel is a 'core' that glows in the dark.
goDifference: In the main series, Staryu needs a Water Stone. In GO, it costs 50 candy to evolve into Starmie.
evolutionCost: 50 candy for Starmie. Buddy distance is 3km/candy.
shinyRate: Shiny Staryu is light blue. Shiny Starmie is light purple.

origin: Starmie is the evolved form of Staryu. Its core glows in 7 different colors. Known for its speed and intelligence, its gems are used as expensive jewelry. Name comes from 'star' + 'mie' (from 'me').
goRelevance: Versatile Water/Psychic type. Runs Water Gun + Hydro Pump + Psychic. Has 210 ATK and high speed. Good for raids and Great League.
battleTip: Starmie with Thunderbolt covers against Water and Flying-types. Electric coverage is rare on Water-types.
easterEgg: Starmie's gem is worth a lot of money. Legend says fishermen who find Starmie become rich by selling its gems.
goDifference: In the main series, Starmie can learn Cosmic Power and Light Screen. In GO, defensive moves work differently.
evolutionCost: 50 Staryu candy. Buddy distance 3km/candy.
shinyRate: Light purple shiny.

origin: Mr. Mime is inspired by mimes and street clowns. A master of mimicry, it can create invisible barriers. Name is 'mister' + 'mime'. Galarian variant is Ice/Psychic.
goRelevance: Regional (Europe). Psychic/Fairy type with Confusion + Psychic + Shadow Ball. Mime Jr. (Gen 4) is obtained from eggs. Galarian Mr. Mime evolves into Mr. Rime.
battleTip: Mr. Mime with Confusion + Psychic + Shadow Ball provides coverage against Ghost-types. Its 205 DEF is high, making it a solid Psychic tank.
easterEgg: Mr. Mime is one of the few Pokémon with a gendered name ('Mr.') that can be female—a contradiction Game Freak has never explained.
goDifference: In the main series, Mr. Mime learns Barrier and Light Screen. In GO, Barrier doesn't exist, so Mr. Mime uses Psychic as a charged move.
evolutionCost: 50 candy from Mime Jr. (5km egg). Mr. Mime does not evolve. Mr. Rime (Galar): 50 candy from Galarian Mr. Mime. 5km buddy distance per candy.
shinyRate: Shiny is gray. Galarian Mr. Mime shiny is dark blue.

origin: Scyther is inspired by praying mantises and samurais. Its scythes can cut through thick trees. Name comes from 'scythe' + 'er' (agent).
goRelevance: Common in grassy areas. Evolves into Scizor (Gen 2, Steel/Bug) with a Metal Coat. Scizor is one of the best Bug/Steel types in the game.
battleTip: Scizor with Fury Cutter + Bullet Punch + Iron Head is great for raids and PvP. Resists 10 different types.
easterEgg: Scyther is one of the few Pokémon that changes its typing upon evolution (Bug/Flying → Bug/Steel). It loses its 4x Rock weakness (4x → 2x).
goDifference: In the main series, Scyther needs a Metal Coat via trade. In GO, it costs 50 Candy + Metal Coat.
evolutionCost: 50 Candy + Metal Coat for Scizor. Buddy 5km/candy. Mega Scizor: 200 Mega Energy.
shinyRate: Light green Shiny. Shiny Scizor is also light green.

origin: Jynx is inspired by opera singers and divas. It wears a red dress and dances to hypnotize. Its name comes from 'jinx' (bad omen).
goRelevance: Rare in icy areas. Ice/Psychic with Confusion + Psychic + Avalanche. Used in Dragon and Flying-type raids.
battleTip: Jynx with Confusion + Avalanche + Psychic covers Fighting, Dragon, and Poison-types. High 223 ATK.
easterEgg: Jynx was redesigned in 2002 after its original design (black skin, large lips) was accused of blackface. It now has purple skin.
goDifference: In the main series, Jynx evolves from Smoochum (Gen 2). In GO, Smoochum hatches from 5km eggs and evolves with 25 candy.
evolutionCost: 25 Smoochum candy (5km egg). Buddy 5km/candy. Jynx does not evolve further.
shinyRate: Light gray shiny. Very subtle.

origin: Electabuzz is inspired by electric demons and yetis. It generates static electricity that makes its fur stand on end. Its name combines 'electric' and 'buzz'.
goRelevance: Common in urban areas. Electivire (Gen 4) is an Electric-type with Thunder Shock + Wild Charge + Ice Punch. Great for raids.
battleTip: Electivire with Ice Punch provides coverage against Ground and Dragon-types—coverage a pure Electric-type lacks. Use it for versatility.
easterEgg: Electabuzz appears near power distribution centers. If the power goes out near a substation, an Electabuzz might be nearby.
goDifference: In the main series, Electabuzz requires a trade with an Electirizer. In GO, it costs 100 candy + a Sinnoh Stone to evolve into Electivire.
evolutionCost: 25 candy from Elekid (5km egg) + 100 candy + Sinnoh Stone for Electivire. Buddy distance is 5km/candy.
shinyRate: Shiny is golden. Shiny Electivire is a darker gold.

origin: Magmar is inspired by fire demons and salamanders. Its body temperature is 1200 degrees, causing the surrounding air to shimmer with heat. Its name comes from 'magma' + 'mar' (possibly from 'mare'—pool?).
goRelevance: Common in volcanic areas. Magmortar (Gen 4) is a Fire-type with access to Fire Spin + Fire Punch + Thunderbolt.
battleTip: Magmortar with Thunderbolt provides coverage against Water-types. Electric-type coverage is rare for Fire-types.
easterEgg: Magmar appears in the anime as the Pokémon that burned a character's father's face. A serious scene that became a meme years later.
goDifference: In the main series, Magmar requires a trade with a Magmarizer. In GO, it costs 100 Candy + a Sinnoh Stone to evolve into Magmortar.
evolutionCost: 25 Candy from Magby (5km egg) + 100 Candy + Sinnoh Stone for Magmortar. Buddy distance is 5km/candy.
shinyRate: Light pink shiny. Shiny Magmortar is also pink.

origin: Pinsir is inspired by beetles and stag beetles. Its pincers are strong enough to cut through logs. The name comes from 'pincer' + 'sir'.
goRelevance: Common in grassy areas. A Bug-type with Fury Cutter + Close Combat + X-Scissor. Mega Pinsir (Bug/Flying) is superior.
battleTip: Mega Pinsir with Fury Cutter + Close Combat + X-Scissor provides coverage against Steel and Rock-types. Mega ATK is 305.
easterEgg: Pinsir and Heracross are the beetle 'rivals'—one is pure Bug, the other Bug/Fighting. Pinsir is region-exclusive in some areas.
goDifference: In the main series, Pinsir can learn Guillotine (OHKO). In GO, there are no OHKO moves.
evolutionCost: Does not evolve. Buddy 3km/candy. Mega Pinsir: 200 Mega Energy.
shinyRate: Dark blue shiny. Mega Pinsir shiny is also blue.

origin: Tauros is inspired by bulls. It runs in herds and is very aggressive. Its name comes from 'tauros' (bull in Greek/Latin). Paldean regional variants exist (3 forms: Fighting, Dark, Water).
goRelevance: North American regional. Normal-type with 198 ATK and 183 DEF. Decent for Great League with Body Slam + Earthquake.
battleTip: Tauros with Body Slam + Earthquake provides coverage against Steel and Rock-types. Body Slam is one of the best Normal-type charged moves.
easterEgg: Tauros is a North American exclusive regional in Pokémon GO. Paldean variants are found in other regions.
goDifference: In the main series, Tauros can learn Thrash naturally. In GO, Thrash does not exist as a move.
evolutionCost: Does not evolve. 3km buddy distance per candy.
shinyRate: Light blue shiny. Shiny variants of the Paldean forms are also available.

origin: Magikarp is inspired by common carp. It is famous for being useless in battle, only learning Splash. Its story of a 'small fish becoming a dragon' comes from Chinese folklore. Its name is 'magic' + 'carp'.
goRelevance: Extremely common near water. Gyarados is one of the strongest Water/Flying types. Magikarp is required to complete the 'Evolve a Magikarp' quest (400 candy).
battleTip: Magikarp is useless. It only exists to evolve into Gyarados (400 candy). Do not use Magikarp in battle under any circumstances.
easterEgg: Magikarp is based on the Chinese legend that carp jumping over the 'Dragon Gate' become dragons. The proof? Magikarp → Gyarados (Water/Dragon? No, Water/Flying).
goDifference: In the main series, Magikarp only learns Splash (no effect). In GO, Splash is an actual fast move! It deals 0 damage.
evolutionCost: 400 candy to evolve into Gyarados. Buddy distance is 1km/candy. It is the most expensive evolution in the game in terms of candy.
shinyRate: Shiny is a bright gold. Shiny Gyarados is RED—one of the best-looking ones.

origin: Gyarados is the evolved form of Magikarp. Inspired by the Chinese legend of carp turning into dragons. It has a destructive temperament and terrorizes coastal cities. Name comes from 'gyrate' + 'rados' (?).
goRelevance: Water/Flying with 237 ATK and 186 DEF. Dragon Breath + Aqua Tail + Crunch. Top-tier for Master League and raids. Mega Gyarados (Dark) is excellent.
battleTip: Gyarados with Dragon Breath + Aqua Tail + Crunch provides coverage against Dragon, Ghost, and Psychic-types. Mega Gyarados (Dark) offers even better coverage.
easterEgg: Gyarados is NOT a Dragon-type; it is Water/Flying. The biggest typing expectation error in history. It should have been Water/Dragon.
goDifference: In the main series, Gyarados mega evolves into Dark-type Mega Gyarados. In GO, Mega Gyarados is Dark/Water, making it great for Psychic and Ghost raids.
evolutionCost: 400 Magikarp candy. 1km/candy buddy distance. 296 XL candy to max out. Mega Gyarados: 200 Mega Energy.
shinyRate: VIBRANT RED shiny. Extremely popular. The red shiny Gyarados is one of the most famous in the franchise.

origin: Lapras is inspired by plesiosaurs and the Loch Ness Monster. It has a blue shell and sings melodies that calm the sea. Its name comes from 'la' (musical note) + 'pras' (?).
goRelevance: Rare in icy areas. Water/Ice type with Ice Shard + Surf + Ice Beam. Great for Dragon-type raids. High STA of 277.
battleTip: Lapras is a Water/Ice tank. Ice Shard + Surf + Ice Beam provides coverage against Dragon, Grass, and Ground types. Good for Master League.
easterEgg: Lapras is the inspiration for the Loch Ness Monster. In the anime, Ash helped a child reunite a Lapras with its family.
goDifference: In the main series, Lapras can learn Perish Song (all faint in 3 turns). In GO, Perish Song does not exist.
evolutionCost: Does not evolve. 5km buddy distance per candy. Rare—appears in 3-star raids and 10km eggs.
shinyRate: Light purple shiny. Very beautiful.

origin: Ditto is inspired by modeling clay and amoebas. It can transform into ANY Pokémon by copying its DNA. Its name comes from 'ditto' (the same).
goRelevance: Rare—disguised as other Pokémon on the map. Essential for trading shiny Legendary Pokémon and for breeding in the main series games.
battleTip: Ditto's Transform copies the opponent's moves and stats. It is only useful if the opponent is strong; against weak opponents, Ditto is also weak.
easterEgg: Ditto can be found disguised as other Pokémon in Pokémon GO. Suspicious appearances of an 'unusual' Pokémon might be Ditto.
goDifference: In the main series, Ditto is essential for breeding. In GO, breeding does not exist. Ditto is used to Transform in raids.
evolutionCost: Does not evolve. Buddy 3km/candy. Cannot be found as shiny in GO (though shiny exists in the main series).
shinyRate: Light blue shiny. In GO, it is NOT available as a shiny (currently).

origin: Eevee is inspired by foxes and rabbits. It is genetically unstable, allowing for multiple evolutions. Its name comes from 'eev' (from 'evolution') + 'ee'. The most versatile Pokémon in the franchise.
goRelevance: Relatively common. It has 8 possible evolutions (Vaporeon, Jolteon, Flareon, Espeon, Umbreon, Leafeon, Glaceon, Sylveon), each with a different typing.
battleTip: Each Eeveelution serves a different purpose: Vaporeon (Water tank), Jolteon (Electric ATK), Umbreon (Dark PvP tank), Sylveon (Fairy DPS). Choose based on your needs.
easterEgg: Eevee is the only Pokémon with 8 evolutions. No other Pokémon has as many evolutionary options. It is the absolute record holder.
goDifference: In the main series, Eevee evolves via elemental stones, friendship, or location. In GO, it requires 25 Candy plus a specific name or Lure Module.
evolutionCost: 25 Candy for any Eeveelution. Buddy distance is 3km/candy. For Espeon/Umbreon: walk 10km together and evolve during the day/night.
shinyRate: Shiny is light gray. Each shiny Eeveelution has a different color and all are highly sought after.

origin: Vaporeon is Eevee's Water-type evolution. Composed mostly of water, it can melt into bodies of water. Its name combines 'vapor' and 'eon' (the Eeveelution suffix).
goRelevance: A Water-type with 205 ATK and 277 STA—a massive HP pool. Runs Water Gun + Aqua Tail + Hydro Pump. Great for raids and PvP.
battleTip: With 277 STA, Vaporeon is a great Water tank. Aqua Tail generates energy faster than Hydro Pump. Use it in Master League.
easterEgg: Vaporeon was the subject of one of the most widespread and inappropriate memes in the Pokémon community (Do not search).
goDifference: In the main series, Vaporeon can learn Acid Armor (raises DEF). In GO, Acid Armor does not exist.
evolutionCost: 25 Eevee Candy. 3km buddy distance per candy.
shinyRate: Shiny Vaporeon is light purple with lilac tones.

origin: Jolteon is Eevee's Electric-type evolution. Its fur accumulates electric charges, and its hairs act like needles that discharge electricity. Name comes from 'jolt' + 'eon'.
goRelevance: Electric-type with 232 ATK. Runs Thunder Shock + Thunderbolt + Volt Switch. Good DPS against Water and Flying-types.
battleTip: Jolteon has a high 232 ATK, making it one of the most offensive Electric-types. However, its 182 DEF and 163 STA make it fragile. It generates energy very quickly with Thunder Shock.
easterEgg: Jolteon is a fan favorite for its 'spiky animal' design—a fox covered in electric quills.
goDifference: In the main series, Jolteon can learn Pin Missile and Agility. In GO, Pin Missile exists but isn't top-tier.
evolutionCost: 25 Eevee candy. Buddy distance is 3km/candy.
shinyRate: Shiny Eevee is light green. Shiny Jolteon is neon yellow.

origin: Flareon is Eevee's Fire-type evolution. It stores flames in an internal pouch and releases them through its mouth. Name comes from 'flare' + 'eon'.
goRelevance: Fire-type with 246 ATK—VERY high. Fire Spin + Flamethrower + Overheat. Good DPS for Steel and Ice raids.
battleTip: Flareon has 246 ATK, the highest among Eeveelutions. Fire Spin + Overheat deals massive damage. But Overheat drops ATK—get a second charged move.
easterEgg: Flareon is the 'poor cousin' of the Eeveelutions in GO due to its limited moveset compared to other Fire-types.
goDifference: In the main series, Flareon can learn Will-O-Wisp and Fire Fang. In GO, Fire Fang is the strongest Fire-type fast move.
evolutionCost: 25 Eevee candy. Buddy 3km/candy.
shinyRate: Light orange shiny. Subtle.

origin: Porygon is an artificial Pokémon made of data and code. Created by scientists as a digital Pokémon prototype, it can inhabit cyberspace. Its name comes from 'polygon' + 'ory' (likely from 'laboratory').
goRelevance: Rare. Evolves into Porygon2 (Gen 2) with an Up-Grade and Porygon-Z (Gen 4) with a Sinnoh Stone + Dubious Disc. Porygon-Z is a Normal-type with 264 ATK.
battleTip: Porygon-Z with Lock-On + Zap Cannon is a devastating combo; Lock-On ensures Zap Cannon hits. Porygon2 with Tri Attack + Discharge is a great tank.
easterEgg: Porygon is INFAMOUS for causing epileptic seizures in the 'Dennō Senshi Porygon' episode in Japan, hospitalizing 685 children. The episode never aired again.
goDifference: In the main series, Porygon requires an Up-Grade and Dubious Disc (via trade). In GO, it costs 25 + 100 + 100 candies, a Sinnoh Stone, and an Up-Grade/Dubious Disc.
evolutionCost: 25 candies + Up-Grade for Porygon2, then 100 candies + Sinnoh Stone + Dubious Disc for Porygon-Z. Buddy distance: 3km/candy.
shinyRate: Shiny is dark blue. Shiny Porygon-Z is purple.

origin: Omanyte is inspired by fossil ammonites. It is a Fossil Pokémon revived from a Helix Fossil. Its name comes from 'ammonite' + 'nyte' (possibly from 'night').
goRelevance: Obtained from fossils (Helix Fossil). Omastar is Rock/Water with 207 ATK and 201 DEF. Used in Great League PvP.
battleTip: Omastar with Mud Shot + Rock Slide + Hydro Pump provides coverage against Fire and Rock-types. Rock Slide is a high energy-generating move.
easterEgg: Omanyte (and the Helix Fossil) became the center of a legend in Twitch Plays Pokémon—the 'Helix Fossil' was worshipped as a deity.
goDifference: In the main series, Omanyte is revived from a fossil. In GO, it is obtained from 7km eggs, raids, and field research.
evolutionCost: 50 candy to evolve into Omastar. Buddy distance is 5km/candy.
shinyRate: Shiny is light blue. Shiny Omastar is purple.

origin: Omastar is the evolved form of Omanyte. It used its tentacles to capture prey and went extinct because its shell became too heavy. The name comes from 'Oma' + 'star'.
goRelevance: Rock/Water type with 207 ATK and 201 DEF—balanced stats. Runs Mud Shot + Rock Slide + Hydro Pump. Used in Great League.
battleTip: Omastar with the legacy move Rock Throw + Rock Slide is better than Mud Shot. If you have one with Rock Throw, keep it.
easterEgg: Omastar went extinct because of its own shell; it became so heavy that it could no longer move. An evolutionary self-destruction.
goDifference: In the main series, Omastar can learn Spike Cannon (an exclusive move). In GO, it does not have Spike Cannon.
evolutionCost: 50 Omanyte candy. 5km buddy distance per candy.
shinyRate: Dark purple shiny.

origin: Kabuto is inspired by trilobite fossils and horseshoe crabs. Revived from a Dome Fossil. Its eyes glow in the dark. 'Kabuto' is Japanese for samurai helmet.
goRelevance: Obtained from Dome Fossils. Kabutops is a Rock/Water type with 220 ATK and 186 DEF. Decent for raids and the Great League.
battleTip: Kabutops with Fury Cutter + Aqua Tail + Rock Slide covers Fire and Rock types. Fury Cutter generates energy very quickly.
easterEgg: Kabutops is one of the oldest fossil Pokemon, based on trilobites that lived 500 million years ago.
goDifference: In the main series, Kabuto is revived from a fossil. In GO, it comes from 7km eggs and raids.
evolutionCost: 50 candy to evolve into Kabutops. Buddy distance is 5km/candy.
shinyRate: Shiny is light pink. Shiny Kabutops is gold.

origin: Kabutops is the evolved form of Kabuto. Its arm blades act like scythes. It hunted prey in the water. Name comes from 'kabuto' + 'tops' (top/end).
goRelevance: Rock/Water type with 220 ATK. Runs Fury Cutter + Aqua Tail + Rock Slide. Decent for PvP due to fast energy generation.
battleTip: Kabutops with the legacy move Mud Shot + Stone Edge was much better. If you have one with Mud Shot, it is valuable.
easterEgg: Kabutops looks like a living fossilized skeleton. Legend says Kabutops was an inspiration for Scyther's design.
goDifference: In the main series, Kabutops can learn Slash and Swords Dance. In GO, Swords Dance is a buff move that Kabutops cannot access.
evolutionCost: 50 Kabuto Candy. 5km buddy distance per candy.
shinyRate: Golden shiny.

origin: Aerodactyl is inspired by pterodactyls and pterosaurs. It is a fossil Pokémon revived from Old Amber. Its bite is powerful enough to crush rocks. Its name comes from 'aero' (air) + 'dactyl' (finger, from pterodactyl).
goRelevance: Rock/Flying with 221 ATK. Rock Throw + Rock Slide + Iron Head. It has a Mega Evolution (Rock/Flying) that is excellent for raids. A great counter for Flying and Ice-types.
battleTip: Mega Aerodactyl with Rock Throw + Rock Slide + Earth Power is top-tier for raids. Its typing is aggressive and covers against Psychic and Ghost types.
easterEgg: Aerodactyl is revived from Old Amber—unlike other fossils which are shells or bones. The amber preserved its DNA.
goDifference: In the main series, Aerodactyl mega evolves. In GO, Mega Aerodactyl is one of the best Rock-types.
evolutionCost: Does not evolve. 5km buddy distance/candy. Obtained from 7km eggs, 3-star raids, and field research. Mega Aerodactyl: 200 Mega Energy.
shinyRate: Shiny is light pink. Mega Aerodactyl shiny is wine-colored.

origin: Snorlax is inspired by hibernating bears and sumo wrestlers. It sleeps constantly, waking only to eat (450kg of food daily). Name comes from 'snore' + 'lax' (relaxed).
goRelevance: Rare. Normal-type with 330 STA — the highest among non-evolutions. Lick + Body Slam + Earthquake. Mega Snorlax is top-tier. Excellent gym defender.
battleTip: Snorlax with Lick + Body Slam (Legacy) + Superpower is top-tier in Master League. Body Slam provides constant pressure. Superpower covers Steel-types.
easterEgg: Snorlax blocks paths in the main series games (the famous 'Snorlax blocking the road'). Requires a Poke Flute to wake up.
goDifference: In the main series, Snorlax can Mega Evolve. In GO, Mega Snorlax has massive STA and is a great tank.
evolutionCost: 25 candy from Munchlax (5km egg). 5km/candy buddy distance. 296 XL candy to max Snorlax to level 50.
shinyRate: Dark blue shiny. Shiny Munchlax is blue.

origin: Articuno is inspired by mythological ice phoenixes and arctic birds. It freezes moisture in the air as it flies. Its name combines 'arctic' and 'uno' (Spanish for one). One of the three legendary birds.
goRelevance: Legendary. Ice/Flying type with Ice Shard + Ice Beam + Hurricane. Great for Dragon and Flying-type raids. Galarian Articuno (Psychic/Flying) is a different form.
battleTip: Articuno with Icy Wind is a great PvP debuffer, lowering the opponent's ATK. Galarian Articuno is rare and only found via Daily Adventure Incense.
easterEgg: Articuno is the first legendary Pokémon Ash encounters in the anime (at Seafoam Islands). It remains one of the most iconic scenes.
goDifference: In the main series, Articuno can learn Sheer Cold (OHKO). In GO, there is no similar OHKO move.
evolutionCost: Does not evolve. Buddy distance: 20km/candy. Appears in 5-star raids (rotation). XL candy: 296 to max out.
shinyRate: Shiny is dark purple. Galarian Articuno shiny is light purple.

origin: Zapdos is inspired by Native American thunderbird mythology. Its wings generate lightning when flapped. Name comes from 'zap' + 'dos' (two). The second of the three legendary birds.
goRelevance: Legendary. Electric/Flying with Thunder Shock + Thunderbolt + Drill Peck. ATK 253 — very high. Galarian Zapdos (Fighting/Flying) is a battle regional.
battleTip: Zapdos with Thunder Shock + Thunderbolt + Drill Peck covers Grass and Fighting-types. Galarian Zapdos is Fighting/Flying and purely melee.
easterEgg: Zapdos is considered the strongest of the three Kanto legendary birds due to its typing coverage and high ATK.
goDifference: In the main series, Zapdos can learn Light Screen and Rain Dance. In GO, weather moves are not implemented the same way.
evolutionCost: Does not evolve. Buddy 20km/candy. 5-star raids. Galarian Zapdos: Daily Adventure Incense (very low catch rate, flees).
shinyRate: Light gold shiny. Galarian Zapdos is dark gold shiny.

origin: Moltres is inspired by the mythological Phoenix. It is reborn from ashes upon death. Its flames are hot enough to melt rock. Its name combines 'molten' and 'tres' (three).
goRelevance: Legendary. Fire/Flying with Fire Spin + Sky Attack + Overheat. 251 ATK. Galarian Moltres (Dark/Flying) is a top-tier Dark-type for PvP.
battleTip: Moltres with Sky Attack provides coverage against Rock and Water-types. Galarian Moltres with Snarl + Fiery Wrath + Brave Bird is top-tier in Ultra League.
easterEgg: Moltres is reborn from ashes when injured—a clear reference to the Phoenix. The name 'Moltres' combines 'molten' with 'tres'.
goDifference: In the main series, Moltres can learn Sunny Day and Will-O-Wisp. In GO, Sunny Day does not exist as a move.
evolutionCost: Does not evolve. 20km buddy distance per candy. Found in 5-star raids. Galarian Moltres: Daily Adventure Incense (flees).
shinyRate: Shiny is light orange. Galarian Moltres shiny is dark purple.

origin: Dratini is inspired by mythological dragons and sea serpents. It is called the 'Mirage Pokémon' due to being rare and elusive. Its name comes from 'dragon' + 'tiny'.
goRelevance: Rare near water. Dragonite is Dragon/Flying with a massive 263 ATK. Runs Dragon Tail + Draco Meteor + Superpower. One of the best Dragons.
battleTip: Dragonite with Dragon Breath + Dragon Claw + Superpower is versatile. Dragon Claw has a low energy cost. Superpower provides coverage against Steel-types.
easterEgg: Dratini was so rare in the original games that it was sold for real money on auction sites. One of the first highly coveted 'shinies'.
goDifference: In the main series, Dratini is caught by fishing with a Super Rod. In GO, it spawns near water but remains rare.
evolutionCost: 25 candy for Dragonair + 100 candy for Dragonite = 125 total. Buddy distance is 5km/candy.
shinyRate: Shiny is light pink. Shiny Dragonite is green.

origin: Dragonair is the middle evolution of Dratini. It has a mystical aura and can control the weather. The blue orbs on its body contain draconic energy. Its name comes from 'dragon' + 'air'.
goRelevance: A stepping stone to Dragonite. Rarely used on its own, but popular for its beauty. Dragonair is one of the most beloved designs.
battleTip: Dragonair is statistically inferior to Dragonite. Always evolve it into Dragonite if you can.
easterEgg: Dragonair is often considered the most beautiful middle-evolution design. Many fans prefer Dragonair's appearance over Dragonite's.
goDifference: In the main series, Dragonair can learn Water Pulse naturally. In GO, Water Pulse is a charged move that Dragonair has access to.
evolutionCost: 100 candy to evolve into Dragonite. Buddy distance is 5km/candy.
shinyRate: Light pink shiny.

origin: Dragonite is the final form of Dratini. Inspired by European dragons. Despite its friendly look, it is extremely powerful. Is the name 'dragon' + 'knight', or 'nite' for night?
goRelevance: Dragon/Flying with 263 ATK. Dragon Breath + Dragon Claw + Superpower. Top-tier for Master League and raids. The best Dragon for versatility.
battleTip: Dragonite with Draco Meteor + Dragon Claw is a classic combo. Use Draco Meteor to nuke, Dragon Claw for pressure. Watch out for Ice-types—they deal 4x damage.
easterEgg: Dragonite is friendly and saves shipwrecked sailors. Its strength is legendary—it can fly around the world in 16 hours.
goDifference: In the main series, Dragonite learns Hyper Beam and Fly. In GO, Hyper Beam is Normal-type; Draco Meteor is better for Dragon STAB.
evolutionCost: 100 Candy from Dragonair. Buddy distance is 5km/candy. Needs 296 XL Candy to max out to level 50.
shinyRate: Shiny Dratini is light green. Shiny Dragonite is dark green.

origin: Mewtwo is inspired by genetic experiments and the movie 'Alien'. It was cloned from Mew in a lab. It is the most powerful Pokémon from Kanto. Its name comes from 'mew' (cat sound) + 'two'.
goRelevance: Legendary. Psychic-type with 300 ATK — the highest in Gen 1. Best moves: Confusion + Psychic + Shadow Ball. Two Mega Evolutions: Mega X (Psychic/Fighting) and Mega Y (Psychic).
battleTip: Mewtwo with Psycho Cut + Psystrike (Legacy) is the best set. Psystrike is a powerful exclusive move. Mega Mewtwo Y has 392 ATK — the highest in the game.
easterEgg: Mewtwo is one of the few Pokémon with TWO Mega Evolutions. Mega X is Fighting/Psychic, while Mega Y is pure Psychic with maximum ATK.
goDifference: In the main series, Mewtwo is found in Cerulean Cave. In GO, it appears in EX Raids and 5-star raids on special rotation.
evolutionCost: Does not evolve. Buddy 20km/candy. 296 XL candy to max out. Mega Mewtwo: 200 Mega Energy per form.
shinyRate: Light green shiny. Subtle but highly coveted.

origin: Mew is inspired by the mythical creature containing the DNA of all Pokémon. It is pink, feline, and floats. It was discovered in South America. Named 'Mew' after the sound a cat makes.
goRelevance: Mythical. Psychic-type with 210 ATK and 210 DEF—perfectly balanced stats. Shadow Claw + Psychic + Ice Beam or Surf. Extremely versatile.
battleTip: Mew is the most versatile Pokémon in GO—it can learn dozens of different Charged Moves. Shadow Claw + Wild Charge + Flamethrower is a popular combo.
easterEgg: Mew was such a well-kept secret in the original games that Nintendo denied its existence. It was only discovered via glitches (Mew Truck, Mew glitch).
goDifference: In the main series, Mew is obtained via special events. In GO, it was distributed through Special Research (A Mythical Discovery) and events.
evolutionCost: Does not evolve. 20km buddy distance per candy. 296 XL Candy to max out. Cannot be placed in Gyms.
shinyRate: Light blue Shiny. EXTREMELY rare—only appeared in special GO events.
